topics.title: Byaheng Ateneo (1872-1877)
topics.text: June 10, 1872 : The date Rizal traveled to Manila with Paciano to take entrance exams at the College of San Juan de Letran. Father Magin Fernando : The {{blank-1}} who initially refused Rizal's admission because he was late and appeared sickly. Manuel Xeres Burgos : The {{blank-2}} of Father Burgos who helped Rizal get admitted. The Rizal Surname : Used officially for the first time to avoid Spanish suspicion surrounding {{blank-3}}. Caraballo Street : Rizal's first boarding house owned by {{blank-4}}. No. 6 Magallanes Street : Rizal's second boarding house inside Intramuros owned by {{blank-5}}. Roman Empire : The student faction of {{blank-6}} (boarders) with red banners. Carthaginian Empire : The student faction of {{blank-7}} (non-boarders) with blue banners. Empire Ranks : The {{blank-8}} hierarchical ranks: Emperor, Tribune, Decurion, Centurion, and Standard-bearer. Rayadillo : The uniform of {{blank-9}} students. Fr. Jose Bech : Rizal's first-year professor (1872-1873). Prophecy : Rizal predicted his mother would be released in {{blank-10}} months. Literary Influences : {{blank-11}} by Alexander Dumas. Father Francisco de Paula Sanchez : Rizal's best professor. March 23, 1877 : The date Rizal graduated with a Bachelor of Arts at age {{blank-12}}. Sacred Heart of Jesus : A sculpture that played a significant role during Rizal's last hours at {{blank-13}}.

topics.title: Byaheng UST (1877-1880)
topics.text: April 1877 : Rizal enrolled in Philosophy and Letters at {{blank-1}}. Shift to Medicine : Done for two reasons: 1) Advice of Ateneo Rector Father {{blank-2}}, 2) To cure his mother's increasing {{blank-3}}. November 25, 1881 : Rizal passed the exam for {{blank-4}}. A La Juventud Filipina (1879) : A poem that won first prize - the first to express the youth as the fair hope of the fatherland. The Council of the Gods (1880) : An allegorical drama that won Rizal a {{blank-5}}. December 8, 1880 : The date the zarzuela {{blank-6}} was staged. Suffocating Atmosphere : How Rizal viewed UST due to Dominican professors and {{blank-7}}. Champion : Rizal founded a secret student society in {{blank-8}} for Filipino unity.

topics.title: Lipad pa-Espanya (1882-1885)
topics.text: Secret Mission : To observe European life and governments to prepare for liberating the Filipino people. May 3, 1882 : The date Rizal departed on the Spanish Steamer {{blank-1}}. June 16, 1882 : Rizal reached Barcelona and wrote {{blank-2}} under the pen name {{blank-3}}. November 3, 1882 : Rizal enrolled at the Universidad Central de {{blank-4}}. Me Piden Versos : A sad poem written for the {{blank-5}}. March 1883 : Rizal joined the Masonic Lodge {{blank-6}} in Madrid under the name Dimasalang. June 17, 1883 : Rizal sojourned to {{blank-7}}. June 25, 1884 : The banquet where Rizal saluted {{blank-8}} (Spoliarium). June 21, 1884 : Rizal was conferred Licentiate in {{blank-9}}. June 19, 1885 : Rizal earned Licentiate in Philosophy and Letters with a rating of {{blank-10}}.

topics.title: Lakbay sa Europa (1885-1887)
topics.text: November 1885 - February 1886 : Rizal worked in Paris as assistant to Dr. {{blank-1}}. February 1886 : Rizal moved to {{blank-2}} to work at the University Eye Hospital. April 22, 1886 : Wrote {{blank-3}}, inspired by flowers of the Neckar River. July 31, 1886 : Rizal began correspondence with Prof. {{blank-4}}. August 1886 : Rizal arrived in Leipzig and translated {{blank-5}} into Tagalog. November 1886 : Rizal arrived in {{blank-6}}. March 21, 1887 : The publication date of {{blank-7}} in Berlin. Maximo Viola : The {{blank-8}} who funded the Noli publication. May 11, 1887 : Start of the Grand Tour of Europe with Maximo Viola. May 13-16, 1887 : Rizal met Prof. {{blank-9}} in Leitmeritz. June 27, 1887 : Rizal visited {{blank-10}}.

topics.title: Balik-Pinas (1887-1888)
topics.text: July 3, 1887 : Rizal left {{blank-1}}, France, to return to the Philippines. August 8, 1887 : Rizal arrived in Calamba with four objectives including performing eye surgery on his mother and defending oppressed countrymen. Doctor Uliman : The nickname for Rizal because he was a doctor from {{blank-2}}. Governor General {{blank-3}} : Summoned Rizal to Malacañang due to the Noli controversy and assigned {{blank-4}} as his bodyguard. Personal Sorrows : Death of his older sister {{blank-5}} and failure to see {{blank-6}}. Groundless Rumors : Enemies labeled him a German spy and agent of {{blank-7}}. February 3, 1888 : Rizal departed via Hong Kong, Macao, Japan, and the {{blank-8}}.
